subcodes.title: Address at Theosophical Society of Paris, 1911-12-06
subcodes.first_line: The supreme virtue of the human world is divine wisdom [divine philosophy] [3.5s]... ...Philosophy consisteth in understanding the reality of things even as they are, and the knowledge of this reality and its comprehension is not possible save through divine philosophy
subcodes.first_line_orig: اعظم فضائل عالم انسانی حکمت الهی است و حکمت عبارت از اطلاع بحقائق اشیا علی ما
subcodes.subjects: @power of the mind over nature, @The Holy Spirit, @prejudice; racial prejudice; class distinction, @man's distinction from the animal, @harmony of science and religion, @unity; oneness of humanity, @proofs for the existence of God, @independent investigation of truth; reality
